steps.title: Condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has infinitely many solutions when both equations represent the exact same line. This requires identical slopes and identical $y$-intercepts.

steps.title: Identify the required slope
steps.content: The given equation $y = \dfrac{2}{9}x + 8$ has slope $\dfrac{2}{9}$. For the system to have infinitely many solutions, the second equation must have the same slope.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-\frac{9}{2}$: Different slope; lines would intersect at exactly one point. ✗
  • Choice B — $-\frac{2}{9}$: Negative of the given slope; lines would intersect at exactly one point. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{2}{9}$: Matches the slope of the given equation. A second equation with this slope and the same $y$-intercept would be identical, giving infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice D — $\frac{9}{2}$: Different slope; lines would intersect at exactly one point. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
